messages.content:
Customer question: what is the refund window for annual plans, and is it different for monthly plans? Below are the top-20 knowledge-base chunks our retriever returned (ranked by embedding similarity). Answer the question and cite the chunk you used.

[chunk 01 · billing/refund-policy.md · similarity 0.84]
Refunds for annual subscriptions: customers may request a full refund within 30 days of the initial charge or any renewal charge. After the 30-day refund window closes, we issue a prorated account credit instead of a refund. Refunds are returned to the original payment method within 5–10 business days.

[chunk 02 · billing/faq.md · similarity 0.79]
Monthly subscriptions have a shorter refund window than annual ones: 14 days from the charge date, after which the subscription simply runs until the end of the paid month. Downgrades from annual to monthly take effect at the next renewal. All refund requests go through the billing portal, not support email.

[chunk 03 · hr/pto-policy.md · similarity 0.41]
Full-time employees accrue 1.67 vacation days per month (20 per year), capped at 30 banked days. PTO requests over five consecutive days need manager approval two weeks in advance. Unused days above the cap do not roll over past January 31.

[chunk 04 · it/vpn-setup.md · similarity 0.39]
To reach internal services from outside the office, install the WireGuard profile from the IT portal and authenticate with your hardware key. Split tunneling is enabled by default; only 10.0.0.0/8 routes through the VPN. Sessions expire after 12 hours.

[chunk 05 · security/training.md · similarity 0.38]
All employees must complete the security-awareness course within 30 days of joining and annually thereafter. Phishing-simulation failures trigger a refresher module. Completion status syncs to the compliance dashboard every night.

[chunk 06 · eng/code-review.md · similarity 0.37]
Every change to a production service needs one approving review from a code owner. Reviews should land within one business day; use the #code-review channel to escalate stale PRs. Draft PRs are exempt from the SLA.

[chunk 07 · eng/on-call.md · similarity 0.37]
The on-call rotation is weekly, Monday 10:00 to Monday 10:00, with a primary and a shadow. Pages must be acknowledged within five minutes. Handoff notes go in the on-call log before the rotation ends.

[chunk 08 · hr/expenses.md · similarity 0.36]
Submit expense reports within 30 days of the purchase date through the finance portal. Meals during travel are capped at 75 USD per day. Receipts are required for any item over 25 USD; card statements are not accepted as receipts.

[chunk 09 · it/laptop-refresh.md · similarity 0.35]
Laptops are refreshed every 36 months or when repair cost exceeds half the replacement price. Request a refresh through the IT portal; standard builds ship within five business days. Old devices must be returned for wiping within two weeks.

[chunk 10 · hr/benefits-401k.md · similarity 0.35]
The company matches 100% of retirement contributions up to 4% of base salary, vesting immediately. Enrollment changes are allowed during onboarding and the November open-enrollment window. Contribution changes take effect the following pay period.

[chunk 11 · ops/incident-severity.md · similarity 0.34]
SEV1 means customer-facing outage or data loss: page the on-call immediately and open a war room. SEV2 is degraded service with a workaround. Postmortems are required for SEV1 and SEV2 within five business days, blameless by policy.

[chunk 12 · eng/deploy-process.md · similarity 0.34]
Production deploys go through the pipeline only — no manual pushes. A deploy needs green CI, an approved PR, and a rollback plan in the description. Deploy freezes are announced in #eng-announce and apply to everything except SEV fixes.

[chunk 13 · hr/interviews.md · similarity 0.33]
Engineering interviews run four rounds: recruiter screen, technical screen, virtual onsite, and team match. Debriefs happen within 48 hours and every interviewer submits written feedback before seeing others' scores.

[chunk 14 · facilities/office-access.md · similarity 0.33]
Badge access works 06:00–22:00 on weekdays; weekend access requires a manager-approved request. Visitors must be registered a day ahead and escorted at all times. Lost badges must be reported to facilities within 24 hours.

[chunk 15 · it/sso-setup.md · similarity 0.32]
All internal applications authenticate through the identity provider with SAML or OIDC; password logins are disabled. New apps must request an integration review from the security team. Sessions last eight hours with silent renewal.

[chunk 16 · marketing/brand.md · similarity 0.32]
Use the primary palette (ink #101418, signal #3B6EF6) for product surfaces and reserve the accent gradient for marketing pages. The wordmark needs clear space equal to its x-height. Never set body copy in the display face.

[chunk 17 · legal/data-retention.md · similarity 0.31]
Customer content is retained for the life of the account plus 90 days, then hard-deleted from primary stores and backups age out within 35 more days. Audit logs are kept for one year. Legal holds pause deletion for named accounts.

[chunk 18 · ops/travel-booking.md · similarity 0.31]
Book all business travel through the travel portal so itineraries sync with expense reports. Economy is standard for flights under six hours. Hotel caps vary by city tier and are enforced at booking time.

[chunk 19 · eng/slack-etiquette.md · similarity 0.30]
Default to public channels; DMs are for sensitive topics only. Use threads to keep channels scannable and @here only for time-sensitive items affecting the whole channel. Set your status when on focus time.

[chunk 20 · facilities/gym-stipend.md · similarity 0.30]
The wellness stipend reimburses up to 50 USD per month for gym memberships or fitness apps, claimed quarterly through the finance portal. Unused months do not roll over. The stipend is taxable in most regions.
